output:
(B).

The scenario describes three classic supply-side responses to partial rent control, all of which compound against new residents:

- **Supply shrinks** (condo conversions remove rental units)
- **Quality deteriorates** (deferred maintenance on controlled units)
- **New-tenant rents overshoot** (landlords cross-subsidize locked-in losses by charging new tenants 20% above counterfactual market rates)

After 5 years, new residents face fewer units, higher prices, and worse quality on what remains — textbook welfare transfer from newcomers to incumbents. The policy achieves the opposite of broad affordability.
